preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 48%, while England's win probability stands at 22%. Argentina enters as clear favorite, though a 29% draw probability indicates this match carries substantial competitive balance despite the gap separating the two outright outcomes.

The spread reveals a moderately wide gap between Argentina's most likely result and England's. Draw odds at nearly 30% suggest the teams are closer in execution than headline win probabilities might imply. A scoreless or tied finish remains plausible; Argentina's advantage lies in frequency of victory rather than dominance of expected margin or margin distribution.

The market and model diverge meaningfully. Sportsbooks place England's win probability 14 percentage points higher than the model, while estimating Argentina's at 17 points lower. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the discrepancy likely reflects different weighting of unmeasured factors or model-specific limitations in capturing team quality at this stage.
